headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: May 9, 2026**
article:
The Toronto Blue Jays dominated the Los Angeles Angels in a 14-1 victory, while the Oakland Athletics secured a 6-2 win over the Baltimore Orioles. The Houston Astros fell to the Cincinnati Reds, and the Miami Marlins came from behind to defeat the Washington Nationals 8-7. The Colorado Rockies suffered a shutout loss to the Philadelphia Phillies.

Injuries and availability continue to impact teams, with the Los Angeles Dodgers placing RHP Brock Stewart on the 15-day injured list due to a left foot bone spur. The San Francisco Giants placed RHP Logan Webb on the 15-day injured list retroactive to May 6, 2026, with right knee bursitis. The Milwaukee Brewers placed LF Brandon Lockridge on the 10-day injured list with a right knee laceration and contusion.

Several trades and roster movements have taken place, including the San Francisco Giants trading C Patrick Bailey to the Cleveland Guardians for LHP Matt Wilkinson and other considerations. The Philadelphia Phillies traded RHP Enyel Garcia and RHP Maicol Lauriano to the Baltimore Orioles for cash. The Athletics traded RHP Tyler Ferguson to the Chicago Cubs, and the Tampa Bay Rays traded RHP Yoendrys Gómez to the Minnesota Twins for cash.

top_news.title: **Blue Jays Rout Angels 14-1**
top_news.summary: The Toronto Blue Jays dominated the Los Angeles Angels in a one-sided matchup, securing a 14-1 victory. Brandon Valenzuela led the Angels' offense with 4 hits, 1 home run, and 4 RBIs.

top_news.title: **Athletics Take Down Orioles 6-2**
top_news.summary: The Oakland Athletics secured a 6-2 victory over the Baltimore Orioles, with Brent Rooker leading the charge at the plate. Aaron Civale earned the win with 5 strong innings, striking out 6 batters and allowing 0 earned runs.

top_news.title: **Astros Fall to Reds in Cincinnati**
top_news.summary: The Houston Astros dropped a 1-3 decision to the Cincinnati Reds, with Braden Shewmake leading the Astros' offense with a home run and an RBI. Spencer Arrighetti pitched 5.2 innings for the Astros, striking out five and allowing one earned run.

top_news.title: **Marlins Come from Behind to Win 8-7**
top_news.summary: The Miami Marlins rallied to take the lead in the late innings, ultimately defeating the Washington Nationals 8-7. Kyle Stowers led the Nationals' offense with a 3-hit, 1-home run performance.

top_news.title: **Nationals Fall Short in Miami**
top_news.summary: The Washington Nationals suffered a 2-5 loss to the Miami Marlins, marking their defeat in the matchup. Impact players for the Nationals included Luis García Jr., Daylen Lile, and Heriberto Hernández.

top_news.title: Rockies Suffer Shutout Loss to Phillies
top_news.summary: The Colorado Rockies fell to the Philadelphia Phillies 6-0, marking their latest defeat. Impact players for the Phillies included Kyle Schwarber, Bryce Harper, and Brandon Marsh, while Cristopher Sánchez had a strong outing on the mound.

top_news.title: **Díaz Shifts to 60-Day IL**
top_news.summary: Los Angeles Dodgers right-handed pitcher Edwin Díaz has been transferred from the 15-day injured list to the 60-day injured list due to right elbow loose bodies.

top_news.title: **Giants Make Key Trade Addition**
top_news.summary: The San Francisco Giants acquired left-handed pitcher Matt Wilkinson from the Cleveland Guardians in exchange for catcher Patrick Bailey.
